name: Chimichangas Supreme
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef into 4 pieces. Place in a 5 to 6-quart pan and add water to cover.
Bring to a boil; skim the foam from the surface.
2. Add the salt, pepper, and garlic. Simmer until tender, approximately 1-1/2
to 2 hours.
3. Drain the beef, reserving half of the broth. When the meat is cool, shred it.
4. Chop 2 of the tomatoes.
5. Melt the shortening in a pan over medium heat. Add the onion and bell pepper
and saute until tender.
6. Add the flour, whisking until no lumps remain, and cook for 2 minutes.
7. Add the shredded beef, reserved broth, chopped tomatoes, and green chiles.
Simmer for 15 minutes.
8. Place about half of the meat mixture in a line down the center of each
tortilla. Fold both ends over 1 to 2 inches; fold one side over the other
and roll up into a neat package. Secure with a toothpick.
9. Deep fry in very hot oil (400°F) until crisp and well browned. Drain on
paper towels.
10. Preheat the broiler. Place the chimichangas on an ovenproof platter or in
a baking pan. Spoon red chili sauce over liberally.
11. Sprinkle with grated cheese and green onion. Place under the broiler until
the cheese melts.
12. Combine the sour cream and whipping cream. Slice the remaining 2 tomatoes.
13. Top the chimichangas with the sour cream mixture and guacamole.
14. Garnish with shredded lettuce, sliced tomato, and olives.
